questions.question: Why was the statue called the Happy Prince?
questions.answer: The statue was called the Happy Prince because when he was alive he lived a happy life in a palace where sorrow was not allowed.

questions.question: Why did the swallow choose the statue as his resting place?
questions.answer: The swallow chose the statue because it was high above the city and had fresh air.

questions.question: What made the swallow curious when he first arrived at the statue?
questions.answer: Drops of water fell on him, and he later realised they were the prince’s tears.

questions.question: Why did the prince ask the swallow to help the seamstress?
questions.answer: The seamstress was poor and her child was sick, so the prince wanted to help them.

questions.question: How did the swallow help the sick boy in the seamstress’s house?
questions.answer: He placed the ruby near the woman and fanned the boy with his wings.

questions.question: Why did the swallow stay with the prince for another night?
questions.answer: He stayed because the prince asked him to help another poor person.

questions.question: Why did the prince give his sapphire to the playwright?
questions.answer: The playwright was cold and hungry and needed money to finish his play.

questions.question: Why did the swallow hesitate to remove the prince’s eye?
questions.answer: He hesitated because he felt pity for the prince.

questions.question: How did the playwright react when he found the sapphire?
questions.answer: He believed it was a gift from an admirer and felt happy.

questions.question: Why did the prince ask the swallow to help the match girl?
questions.answer: Because her matches were spoiled and she would be punished by her father.

questions.question: Why did the swallow decide not to go to Egypt?
questions.answer: He decided to stay because he loved the prince and wanted to help him.

questions.question: What did the swallow see when he flew around the city?
questions.answer: He saw rich people enjoying themselves and poor people suffering.

questions.question: How did the prince help the poor people in the city?
questions.answer: He asked the swallow to remove the gold leaves and give them to the poor.

questions.question: What happened to the statue after all the gold leaves were removed?
questions.answer: The statue became dull and grey.

questions.question: How did the poor children react after receiving the gold?
questions.answer: They became happy and cheerful because they now had bread.

questions.question: Why did the swallow grow weaker during winter?
questions.answer: Because the weather was very cold and he had little food.

questions.question: What was the swallow’s last request before dying?
questions.answer: He asked permission to kiss the prince’s hand.

questions.question: Why did the prince ask the swallow to kiss him on the lips?
questions.answer: Because he loved the swallow for his kindness.

questions.question: What happened when the swallow died?
questions.answer: The prince’s lead heart broke into two pieces.

questions.question: Why did the Mayor criticize the statue?
questions.answer: Because it had lost its gold and jewels.

questions.question: Why was the statue pulled down?
questions.answer: Because it was no longer considered beautiful.

questions.question: What happened to the statue after it was removed?
questions.answer: It was melted in a furnace.

questions.question: Why could the lead heart not melt?
questions.answer: Because it was very hard and broken.

questions.question: What did the angel bring to God?
questions.answer: The lead heart and the dead swallow.

questions.question: What is the main theme of the story?
questions.answer: The importance of kindness, sacrifice, and compassion.
